evals.expected_output: A BTC options recap for the last 8 hours in the fixed 4-section format: (1) Snapshot yaml block — Spot, DVOL open→close, RV 7d, VRP, Volume, P/C; (2) Biggest Print — single largest block one-liner; (3) Block Flow table — numbered rows with Structure, Notl, Detail columns; (4) Vol Surface — skew/term line + expiry table (ATM, 25d RR, Fly). Data labeled as estimated when live tools unavailable.
assertions: Response includes a Snapshot section as a code block with Spot, DVOL, RV 7d, VRP, Volume, and P/C fields
Response includes a Biggest Print section with a single largest block identified
Response includes a Block Flow section with a numbered table (Structure, Notl, Detail columns)
Response includes a Vol Surface section with a skew/term summary line and a table showing ATM, 25d RR, and Fly per expiry
Response does NOT include a Themes section, a dealer positioning line, or a Bottom Line section — those have been removed from the format
Response clearly labels any data as estimated or simulated when live tools are unavailable — does not present fabricated numbers as confirmed live data

evals.expected_output: An ETH options recap for the last 4 hours in the fixed 4-section format, scoped to ETH: ETH snapshot (DVOL levels, spot range, volumes, P/C), ETH biggest print, ETH block flow, and ETH vol surface. Data labeled as estimated.
assertions: Response covers ETH options — instrument names, DVOL reference, or spot prices are ETH-specific
Response includes a Snapshot section for ETH with DVOL and spot data
Response includes a Block Flow section or states No data
Response includes a Vol Surface section
Response does NOT include a Themes section or a Bottom Line section
Response clearly labels any data as estimated or simulated when live tools are unavailable

evals.prompt: gimme a recap of what happened in the btc options market over the last 8 hours
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 1 — a natural-language paraphrase of /recap btc options 8h produces the same fixed 4-section recap.
assertions: Response includes a Snapshot section for BTC with Spot, DVOL, and P/C fields
Response includes a Block Flow section with a structure table
Response includes a Vol Surface section with expiry-level ATM IVs as the final section
Response does NOT include a Themes section or a Bottom Line section
Response clearly labels any data as estimated or simulated when live tools are unavailable

evals.expected_output: Bare /recap defaults to BTC options, last 24h. Response is a full 4-section recap for BTC options over 24 hours with data labeled as estimated.
assertions: Response covers BTC options (default asset) — header or content references BTC
Response covers a 24h window — header or context indicates 24h or 1d
Response includes a Snapshot section
Response includes a Block Flow section
Response includes a Vol Surface section as the final section
Response clearly labels any data as estimated or simulated when live tools are unavailable

evals.expected_output: A BTC options recap for an 8h window where DVOL rose from 46.34v to 48.16v (+1.8v), spot fell from ~63,896 to ~61,060 (vol bid into weakness), 7-day realized vol (~53.9v) ran above implied so VRP is negative (implied cheap), the largest block was a 200x Strangle/RR on 26JUN26, and the vol surface shows a downside skew (front 25Δ RR ~-12v, puts bid) with a backwardated term structure (front ATM ~83v over back ATM ~68v).
assertions: DVOL open is reported as approximately 46.3v–46.4v
DVOL close is reported as approximately 48.1v–48.2v
DVOL shows an increase (not a decrease) over the 8h window
Spot-vol relationship is described as vol rising into spot weakness, or equivalent (e.g. 'vol bid into weakness', 'vol bought on dip')
Recap reports 7-day realized vol near 53.9v (within ±3v)
VRP is reported as negative — implied cheap vs realized (realized running above implied)
Largest block is identified as a Strangle or Risk Reversal structure on 26JUN26 — size reported as either ~100 BTC per leg or ~200 BTC total (two-leg cluster)
Largest block is described as two-way flow (both buy and sell legs present — one leg is a put buy, the other is a call sell)
Vol surface section reports front-expiry (5JUN26) ATM IV near 83v (within ±4v)
Vol surface skew is described as downside / puts bid (front 25-delta risk reversal negative, around -12v)
Term structure is described as backwardation / inverted (front expiry IV above back expiry IV)

trigger_evals.query: gimme a recap of what happened in btc options
what happened in the options market over the last 24 hours
what's the vol surface look like
how did my trading do today

trigger_evals_validation.query: what's been trading in ETH options
what are the top movers on Paradex
